# A voter goes down. The leader engages the witness to maintain commit quorum,
# then later releases it and transitions back to voter-based commits.
#
# Two voters, one witness. Quorum = 2.

add-voters
v1: [a1]
v2: [a1]
w
----
v1: [a1] term=a cfg=(v1 v2 w)
v2: [a1] term=a cfg=(v1 v2 w)

# v1 wins election. Witness vote is dropped to keep setup minimal.
campaign v1 drop=(w)
----
v1 campaigns at term b
v1: yes (self)
v2: yes
w:  (dropped)
outcome: won

# v1 proposes an entry. v2 is down (partitioned from here on).
# Matches: [v1=3, v2=0, w=0]. Quorum match = 0, not committed.
propose v1
----
v1: [a1 b2 b3] term=b cfg=(v1 v2 w) leader
v2: [a1] term=b cfg=(v1 v2 w)

append v1 drop=(v2)
----
v1 appends through b3
v2: (dropped)
v1: [a1 b2 b3] term=b cfg=(v1 v2 w) leader
v2: [a1] term=b cfg=(v1 v2 w)

# v1 engages the witness. Now it counts toward quorum.
# Matches: [v1=3, v2=0, w=3]. Quorum match = 3, committed!
engage v1
----
ok
w:  vote=(b, -) acked=(b3, engaged)
v1: [a1 b2 b3] term=b cfg=(v1 v2 w) committed=b3 leader

# Same append as before — but now with the witness engaged, committed advances.
append v1 drop=(v2)
----
v1 appends through b3
v2: (dropped)
v1: [a1 b2 b3] term=b cfg=(v1 v2 w) committed=b3 leader
v2: [a1] term=b cfg=(v1 v2 w)

# v2 tries to campaign (v1 partitioned away). The witness rejects: v2's last
# log entry a1 is from term a, which doesn't prove any leader was elected after
# the term b leader that engaged the witness. Without that proof, granting a
# vote could let v2 form a quorum that misses entries the witness helped commit.
campaign v2 drop=(v1)
----
v2 campaigns at term c
v2: yes (self)
v1: (dropped)
w:  no
outcome: lost

# v1 releases the witness, returning to voter-based commits.
release v1
----
ok
w:  vote=(b, -) acked=(b3, released)

# v2 tries again after the release. The witness is no longer engaged, but its
# acked high-water mark b3 is still ahead of v2's last log entry a1. The
# witness won't vote for a candidate whose log doesn't cover what it has acked.
campaign v2 drop=(v1)
----
v2 campaigns at term d
v2: yes (self)
v1: (dropped)
w:  no
outcome: lost

# v1 re-campaigns to catch up to v2's term bumps. v2 rejects (v1's campaign
# term c is stale compared to v2's term d), but the witness grants.
campaign v1
----
v1 campaigns at term c
v1: yes (self)
v2: no, stale term c < d
w:  yes
outcome: won

# v2 comes back online. v1 proposes and replicates to v2, committing via the
# traditional v1+v2 quorum — no witness engagement needed.
propose v1
----
v1: [a1 b2 b3 c4 c5] term=c cfg=(v1 v2 w) leader
v2: [a1] term=d cfg=(v1 v2 w)

append v1
----
v1 appends through c5
v2: ok
v1: [a1 b2 b3 c4 c5] term=c cfg=(v1 v2 w) committed=c5 leader
v2: [a1 b2 b3 c4 c5] term=d cfg=(v1 v2 w)
